title: The 62% Gap - Same Job, Different Paycheck

sources.title: PwC 2026 Global AI Jobs Barometer - press release

claims.claim: PwC's 2026 Global AI Jobs Barometer analysed more than one billion job ads across six continents.
Workers with AI skills command an average wage premium of 62%, up from 57% last year.
The AI skills wage premium runs as high as 118% in sectors such as consumer markets.

scenes.narration: Two people. Same job title. One earns 62% more.

visual.headline: Same job. Not the same paycheck.

visualAssets.editorialPurpose: Hero footage pekerja kantor untuk hook - mendukung klaim gap 62% di dunia kerja nyata

fetch.query: busy office workers typing laptop documents

scenes.narration: PwC just analyzed nearly a billion job ads. The difference isn't experience.

visual.label: higher pay for workers who command AI
visual.subtitle: From more than 1 billion job ads worldwide.

scenes.narration: AI is splitting every job into two tracks. One track just uses AI. Those wages are stalling.

visual.subtitle: One track is replaceable. One is not.

visualAssets.editorialPurpose: Backdrop halus scene perbandingan - mendukung klaim premi gaji 62% bagi pekerja yang belajar skill AI

fetch.query: person taking notes laptop studying online course

scenes.narration: The other track commands AI. Judgment, not just prompts. In some industries, that premium hits 118%.

visual.subtitle: In some industries the premium hits 118%.

scenes.narration: Same title on paper. Completely different paycheck. So if two people share your job title... which one are you?

visual.question: WHICH ONE ARE YOU?
visual.subtitle: The gap isn't experience. It's judgment.

visual.closingLine: Command the machine. Own the premium.

packaging.youtubeTitle: Same Job Title. 62% More Pay. Here's the Skill Gap.
packaging.instagramCaption:
Same job title. 62% more pay. 🤯

PwC just analyzed more than a BILLION job ads. The finding: AI is splitting every career into two tracks —

🔸 Track 1 uses AI for routine work → wages stalling
🔸 Track 2 commands AI with judgment → wages up 62% (118% in some industries)

The gap isn't experience. It isn't even coding. It's judgment.

Which track are you on? 👇

Source: PwC 2026 Global AI Jobs Barometer
